title: Bradleia mollis
intro: Bradleia mollis, also known as Maire's woodruff or New Zealand woodruff, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and provide relief from gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Bradleia mollis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Bradleia mollis belongs to the family Ericaceae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plant species. The genus Bradleia is a group of flowering plants native to New Zealand and Australia. Ericaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the heath family, which includes many species of shrubs and small trees. The classification of Bradleia mollis within Ericaceae highlights its evolutionary relationships with other heath family plants.
